2606.27959.title: An Empirical Analysis of Factual Errors in Human-Written Text and its Application
2606.27959.paper_summary: Factual Error Detection (FED), which is the task of identifying factually incorrect spans in a given text, has long been recognized as an important research problem. However, with the rapid rise of large language models (LLMs), research attention has shifted toward factual errors specific to LLM-generated text (hallucinations) and their detection. As a result, the detection of factual errors in human-written text has been relatively neglected. To address this gap, we first distill a taxonomy of human-induced factual errors by analyzing corrections of newspaper articles, a representative source of text that is guaranteed to be human-written and contains few grammatical errors. Our analysis revealed that there are characteristic categories such as kanji misconversions and numeral classifier errors, which are not focused in existing hallucination benchmarks. Based on the taxonomy, we then evaluate the FED capability of vanilla LLMs on synthesized realistic test cases and real corrections. Experimental results demonstrated that even high-performance LLMs such as GPT-5.4 achieved only word-level F1 score of 52% on the synthetic evaluation data, highlighting the task difficulty. Furthermore, a detailed analysis by detection difficulty revealed the current state of FED.
2606.27959.paper_summary_zh: 事實錯誤檢測（FED），即識別給定文本中事實上不正確的片段的任務，長期以來被認為是一個重要的研究問題。<lb/>然而，隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）的快速崛起，研究重點已轉向特定於LLM生成文本的事實錯誤（幻覺）及其檢測。<lb/>因此，人類撰寫文本中的事實錯誤檢測相對被忽視。<lb/>為了解決這一空白，我們首先通過分析報紙文章的更正，提煉出人為引起的事實錯誤的分類法，這是一個保證為人類撰寫且幾乎不含語法錯誤的文本代表性來源。<lb/>我們的分析顯示出一些特徵類別，例如漢字誤轉換和數量詞錯誤，這些在現有的幻覺基準中並未受到重視。<lb/>基於這一分類法，我們接著評估了普通LLMs在合成的現實測試案例和實際更正上的FED能力。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，即使是高性能的LLMs，如GPT-5.4，在合成評估數據上的字級F1分數僅達到52%，突顯了任務的困難性。<lb/>此外，通過檢測難度的詳細分析揭示了FED的當前狀態。
2606.27959.author: Kazuma Iwamoto et.al.
2606.27959.authors: Kazuma Iwamoto, Kazumasa Omura, Shotaro Ishihara
